message:
fix(cass): plug clipboard fork-hang + structured-mode prompt leak

Self-audit pass on the recent --clipboard / cass upgrade work caught
two bugs that the existing tests didn't surface:

1. Clipboard helper hung indefinitely on real Linux desktops.

   wl-copy / xclip / xsel all daemonize after reading stdin: parent
   process exits, child inherits the parent's stdout / stderr fds and
   holds them open for the lifetime of the X11 / Wayland selection.
   The previous helper used `wait_with_output()`, which reads those
   pipes to EOF — and EOF never arrives until the daemon dies, which
   could be hours later. The helper would block until ctrl-c.

   The original smoke tests passed because this VPS has no Wayland
   compositor and no X display, so wl-copy / xclip both errored out
   *before* forking; the happy path was never exercised. On a real
   developer's macOS / Linux desktop running `cass export -c`, the
   command would hang silently.

   Fix: redirect the child's stdout / stderr to /dev/null instead of
   piping them, and use `wait()` instead of `wait_with_output()`.
   `wait()` returns as soon as the parent exits — which it does
   immediately after forking the daemon — so we no longer block on
   pipe EOF. We give up the rich stderr-on-failure detail in
   exchange, but exit status alone is enough to retry the next
   candidate (and clipboard tools rarely fail in interesting ways).
   Stdin handling now scopes the write+drop in its own block so the
   pipe write end closes before `wait`, preventing any chance of the
   child sitting in `read(stdin)` forever.

2. cass upgrade in --json + TTY + no --yes mixed JSON and y/N prompt.

   The structured-mode return guard was
   `if check_only || !info.is_newer || (!yes && !io::stdin().is_terminal())`.
   That correctly returned for "no update", "--check-only", and
   "no-tty + no-yes", but it fell through to the install path on
   "tty + no-yes" — which then ran `print!("...y/N: ")` *after* the
   JSON envelope had already gone to stdout. Result: a JSON consumer
   piping `cass upgrade --json | jq` would see a JSON line followed
   by an interactive prompt, breaking pipelines.

   Fix: in structured mode, only proceed past JSON emission when the
   caller passed `--yes` (i.e. they explicitly opted into the
   side-effect). Any other branch returns after emitting JSON.
   Documented inline so a future reader understands why we don't
   mirror the prompt-then-install path that non-structured mode uses.

Verification:
- cargo build clean.
- Existing `clipboard_helper_tests::returns_err_when_no_tool_is_available`
  still passes (the PATH-cleared headless path still errors out).
- cargo test --lib 4141 / 0 / 3 — no regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
